question_text: In a parallel circuit with three identical bulbs, if one bulb burns out, what happens to the other two bulbs?

options.label: B
options.text: They both stay at the same brightness as before

options.label: D
options.text: One stays on and one goes dimmer

explanation: In a parallel circuit, each bulb has its own separate path for current. If one bulb burns out, it only breaks that branch, so the other branches remain unaffected and the bulbs stay the same brightness.
